attractions.name: APEX Museum Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.description: The APEX Museum (African American Panoramic Experience) is the oldest Black history museum in Atlanta, founded in 1978. Located in the historic Sweet Auburn District, it interprets history through a Black perspective, covering everything from ancient African civilizations to the local history of the Sweet Auburn neighborhood Official Website, Wikipedia.
attractions.address: 135 Auburn Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30303-2567 Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Sunday – Monday: Closed Official Website
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday Official Website
attractions.admission: Adult: $12; Seniors/Students/Veterans: $8; Children (4 & under): Free; Member: Free Official Website
highlights: 'Pillars of Empowerment: An exhibit honoring the voices and visions of influential Black women [Official Website', 'Yates & Milton Drug Store: A recreated meeting place that was a staple of historic Auburn Avenue Official Website', 'Trolley Theater: Features informative videos on African history and the origins of the slave trade Tripadvisor', 'African Diaspora Exhibits: Dedicated displays telling the rich and often untold stories of people of African descent Official Website', 'Historic John Wesley Dobbs Building: The museum is housed in this 100-year-old structure built by African American masons Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Last entry is at 2:00 PM. Visitors are encouraged to combine their trip with a visit to the nearby MLK National Historical Park. Paid parking is available in the lot adjacent to the museum, and it is also MARTA accessible via the #3 Bus from Five Points station Official Website, Tripadvisor.
attractions.nearby: Martin Luther King, Jr. National Historical Park, Auburn Avenue Research Library, Ebenezer Baptist Church, Sweet Auburn Curb Market, Georgia Aquarium Tripadvisor, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Agnes Scott College
attractions.description: Founded in 1889, Agnes Scott College is a private women's liberal arts college dedicated to empowering students through its innovative SUMMIT curriculum. The campus is renowned for its stunning Gothic-style architecture and is recognized as a leader in global learning and leadership development. It consistently ranks as one of the most innovative liberal arts colleges in the United States. Agnes Scott About, Wikipedia

attractions.admission: Free for campus visits, tours, and Bradley Observatory open houses Agnes Scott Admission, Bradley Observatory

attractions.tips: Visitors to the library should sign the log at the Check Out Desk and adhere to public hours. It is recommended to register online for student-led tours or request a self-guided tour map to explore the historic campus grounds. Agnes Scott Library, Agnes Scott Admission

attractions.name: Atlanta Preservation Center (LP Grant Mansion)
attractions.description: The Atlanta Preservation Center is a non-profit organization dedicated to promoting the preservation of Atlanta’s architecturally, historically, and culturally significant buildings, neighborhoods, and landscapes. Founded in 1979, it operates out of the historic 1856 L.P. Grant Mansion and offers educational programs, advocacy efforts, and guided walking tours that connect participants with the city's rich heritage. Atlanta Preservation Center
attractions.address: 327 St Paul Ave SE, Atlanta, GA 30312
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM, Tuesday: 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM, Wednesday: 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM, Thursday: 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM, Friday: 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM, Saturday: Closed (Tours vary), Sunday: Closed (Tours vary). Atlanta Preservation Center Contact
attractions.admission: Adults: $13, Seniors (60+): $7, Students: $7, Members: Free. (Prices vary slightly by tour, ranging from free to approximately $25 for specialized tours). Explore Georgia, Eventbrite
highlights: ['Guided walking tours of historic neighborhoods like Inman Park, Sweet Auburn, and Druid Hills.', "Headquarters located in the L.P. Grant Mansion, Atlanta's oldest surviving home (built 1856).", 'Phoenix Flies: An annual city-wide celebration featuring free tours of historic sites.', 'Advocacy for the preservation of significant landmarks like the 148 Edgewood Avenue building.', 'Educational partnerships with local schools ranging from pre-school to post-graduate levels.', 'Birthplace of legendary golfer Bobby Jones, located within the L.P. Grant Mansion.']
attractions.tips: Check the official Eventbrite page for the current tour schedule and to purchase tickets in advance, as many tours have limited capacity. Eventbrite, Explore Georgia

attractions.name: Chattahoochee Bend State Park
attractions.description: Chattahoochee Bend State Park is one of Georgia's largest state parks, protecting five miles of river frontage and over 2,900 acres of wilderness. It offers a variety of outdoor activities including paddling, hiking, and camping in a spectacular tract of wilderness in northwest Coweta County. Georgia State Parks
attractions.hours: Park: 7 a.m. – 10 p.m.; Office: 8 a.m. – 5 p.m. Georgia State Parks
attractions.admission: Daily ParkPass: $10 per vehicle; Annual ParkPass: $70 (50% discount for seniors 62+, 25% discount for active military and veterans). Georgia State Parks
highlights: ['Over 12 miles of hiking trails, including the scenic Riverside and Bend Trails', '4.3 miles of mountain biking trails', 'Paddling and boating access to the Chattahoochee River with a boat ramp and kayak launch', 'Unique camping options including cottages, platform campsites, and backcountry sites', 'Two-story observation tower offering panoramic views of the river and forest', 'Excellent fishing and geocaching opportunities within the park']
attractions.tips: The park does not provide a shuttle service for paddlers, so bringing two vehicles is recommended for river trips. Camping reservations are required, and backcountry campers must check in by 1:00 p.m. to reach their sites before dark. Swimming is generally not recommended due to river size and currents. Georgia State Parks, Nate Shivar
attractions.nearby: Atlanta Motor Speedway, Callaway Gardens, F.D. Roosevelt State Park, Roosevelt's Little White House State Historic Site, Joe Kurz WMA. Georgia State Parks

attractions.name: Chattahoochee River National Recreation Area (NPS)
attractions.description: The Chattahoochee River National Recreation Area preserves a 48-mile stretch of the river, offering a green sanctuary for the metro Atlanta area. Established in 1978, the park provides a variety of outdoor activities including hiking along scenic bluffs, fishing in stocked trout waters, and paddling through rocky shoals (NPS, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 1978 Island Ford Parkway, Sandy Springs, GA 30350 (NPS)
attractions.hours: Park Lands: Dawn to Dusk daily; Island Ford Visitor Center: Mon-Sun 9 AM - 5 PM (NPS)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Visitor Center closed Thanksgiving and Christmas Day) (NPS)
attractions.admission: Daily Pass: $5.00, Annual Pass: $40.00, America the Beautiful Pass: Free with valid pass (NPS)
highlights: 'Floating (tubing, rafting, kayaking) from Powers Island to Paces Mill ([Wikipedia)', 'Cochran Shoals fitness trail, a popular 3.5-mile loop for runners and cyclists (Discover Atlanta)', 'East Palisades bamboo forest and stunning river overlooks (Discover Atlanta)', 'Sope Creek historic ruins of the Marietta Paper Mill (Wikipedia)', 'Island Ford Visitor Center located in the historic Hewlett Lodge (NPS)', 'World-class trout fishing in the cold-water river (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: The park is cashless, so be prepared to pay for passes via credit card or digital pass online. For safety, always check the water release schedule from Buford Dam before entering the river, as water levels can rise rapidly (NPS, NPS Safety).
attractions.nearby: Chattahoochee Nature Center, Morgan Falls Overlook Park, Steel Canyon Golf Club (Discover Atlanta, TripAdvisor)

attractions.name: Children's Museum of Atlanta
attractions.description: The Children's Museum of Atlanta is a nonprofit educational venue and the only museum of its kind in the city, specifically designed for children ages 0-8. Through interactive permanent learning zones and rotating featured exhibits, the museum aims to spark every child's imagination and discovery through the power of play Children's Museum of Atlanta. It serves as a premier source of hands-on exploratory learning and a signature element of Atlanta’s cultural landscape Children's Museum of Atlanta.
attractions.address: 275 Centennial Olympic Park Dr NW, Atlanta, GA 30313
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Wednesday: CLOSED; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Saturday: 9:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Sunday: 9:30 AM - 4:30 PM (Members-Only Hour: 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri) Children's Museum of Atlanta

Gateway to the World: Hands-on exploration of global geography and cultures Children's Museum of Atlanta

Let Your Creativity Flow: Art studio where kids can paint on walls and engage in creative projects Children's Museum of Atlanta
Step Up to Science: Interactive science lab exploring biology, physics, and cause-and-effect Children's Museum of Atlanta
Tools for Solutions: Engineering-focused area where children use tools to solve construction challenges Children's Museum of Atlanta

attractions.name: Clark Atlanta University
attractions.description: Clark Atlanta University (CAU) is a private, historically black research university and a leading institution in the Atlanta University Center. Formed by the 1988 consolidation of Atlanta University and Clark College, it is known for its high research activity and deep legacy in African American higher education.

attractions.name: Clermont Lounge
attractions.description: The Clermont Lounge is Atlanta's first and longest continually operating strip club, established in 1965 in the basement of the historic Hotel Clermont (Wikipedia). This legendary dive bar is famous for its kitschy, judgment-free atmosphere, diverse performers of all ages, and status as a favorite haunt for visiting celebrities (Clermont Lounge). It remains one of the city's most iconic and enduring nightlife landmarks, known for its grit and unique charm (Garden & Gun).
attractions.address: 789 Ponce De Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30306
attractions.hours: Monday - Wednesday: 8:00 PM - 3:00 AM; Thursday - Saturday: 3:00 PM - 3:00 AM; Sunday: Closed (Clermont Lounge, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: $10 Cover Fee (Cash only) (Tripadvisor)
highlights: "Atlanta's oldest strip club and a world-renowned dive bar destination ([Clermont Lounge).", 'Features a diverse cast of performers ranging in age from 20 to over 70, including the iconic Blondie (Wikipedia).', 'Famous for hosting celebrities such as Bill Murray, Anthony Bourdain, and Lady Gaga (Clermont Lounge).', 'Strict "No Cameras" policy to ensure a private and authentic experience for all (Clermont Lounge).', 'Hosted in the basement of the 1924 Hotel Clermont, a National Register of Historic Places site (Wikipedia).', 'Signature weekly events include Tuesday Karaoke Night and Disco Saturdays (Clermont Lounge).']
attractions.tips: The establishment is strictly cash only; bring cash beforehand to avoid high ATM fees (Tripadvisor). The entrance is located at the rear of the Hotel Clermont building near the valet area (Garden & Gun). Respect the "no cameras" rule, as security is very strict about phone usage inside (Clermont Lounge). Admission is usually a $10 cover charge, especially after 8:00 PM (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Hotel Clermont Rooftop Bar, Ponce City Market, Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail, Two Urban Licks, Virginia-Highland neighborhood (Tripadvisor, Waze)

It curates a vibrant, city-wide "arts district" experience that includes massive murals, rotating sculptures, and hidden miniature installations. (Decatur Arts Alliance, Visit Decatur Georgia)

attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Visit Decatur Georgia, Decatur Arts Alliance)
attractions.admission: Free (Explore Georgia, Decatur Arts Alliance)
highlights: 'Decatur Arts Festival: A major annual May event featuring an artist market, live music, and performing arts. attractions.name: Decatur Square (also known as Decatur Town Square or The Square)
attractions.description: Decatur Square is the historic and civic heart of the City of Decatur, featuring a large pedestrian plaza anchored by the Historic DeKalb Courthouse and a MARTA station. It serves as a vibrant community hub surrounded by over 45 independent shops, award-winning restaurants, and public art, making it a premier destination for festivals and outdoor gatherings. Visit Decatur GA, PPS.org

attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Individual business hours vary) Kupi.com, Tripadvisor

attractions.tips: Parking can be difficult; use the MARTA Blue Line (Decatur Station) for the easiest access. The courthouse parking deck is free after 5 PM on weekdays and all day on weekends. Visit on Friday evenings or during weekend festivals for the most lively atmosphere. Tripadvisor, Yelp

attractions.name: Druid Hills Historic District (including Druid Hills Parks and Parkways)
attractions.description: The Druid Hills Historic District is a premier residential community and parkway system designed by Frederick Law Olmsted, often called the 'father of American landscape architecture.' Its centerpiece is the curvilinear Ponce de Leon Avenue, which features a 'necklace' of six linear parks and is lined with grand early 20th-century mansions.(The Cultural Landscape Foundation, Wikipedia))
attractions.address: Ponce de Leon Avenue (roughly between Briarcliff Road and the Seaboard Coast Line RR tracks), Atlanta, GA 30306
attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk (Parks); Open 24 hours (District Streets) (Druid Hills Civic Association)
attractions.admission: Free (Druid Hills Civic Association)
highlights: 'Olmsted Linear Park: A series of six interconnected parks (Springdale, Virgilee, Brightwood, Shadyside, Dellwood, and Deepdene) along the avenue ([The Cultural Landscape Foundation)', 'Historic Mansions: Notable estates such as the Callanwolde Fine Arts Center and the former mansion of Coca-Cola founder Asa Candler (Wikipedia))', 'Architectural Diversity: Features styles including Georgian, Tudor, Italian Renaissance, and Craftsman bungalows (Molly Carter Gaines)', 'Fernbank Museum of Natural History: Located within the historic context of the neighborhood (Explore Georgia)', 'The BeltLine Connection: The historic corridor intersects with the modern Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: The best way to experience the district is by walking or cycling through the Olmsted Linear Parks. Visit in the spring to see the neighborhood's famous azaleas and dogwoods in full bloom. (Explore Georgia)
attractions.nearby: Fernbank Museum of Natural History, Ponce City Market, Virginia-Highland neighborhood, Emory University, Little Five Points (Explore Georgia)

attractions.name: Emory University (Atlanta Campus)
attractions.description: Emory University is a prestigious private research university located in the historic Druid Hills neighborhood of Atlanta, Georgia. Founded in 1836, the 600-acre campus is renowned for its Italian Renaissance-style architecture, marble-clad buildings, and lush, forested landscape. It serves as a major cultural and educational hub, featuring prominent institutions like the Michael C. Carlos Museum and the Lullwater Preserve. Emory University, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM (Based on Student Center hours; grounds are generally accessible 24/7) Emory Student Center

Carlos Museum (premier art and archaeology museum)', 'Lullwater Preserve (scenic trails and waterfalls)', 'Dooley Statue (spirit of Emory)', 'Asbury Circle (home to Wonderful Wednesday celebrations)']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable shoes for the hilly, 600-acre campus. Download the Emory visitor app for an interactive self-guided tour experience. Visitors should park in the Oxford Road or Fishburne decks, where fees apply during weekdays. Visit Emory
attractions.nearby: David J. Sencer CDC Museum, Fernbank Museum of Natural History, Virginia-Highland, Piedmont Park Discover Atlanta, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Fernbank Museum of Natural History
attractions.description: The Fernbank Museum of Natural History is a premier science and nature center that explores the natural world through immersive exhibits and massive dinosaur displays. It features 75 acres of outdoor experiences, including a canopy walk and one of the largest old-growth urban Piedmont forests in the country. Visitors can journey from the prehistoric era to the modern day through a variety of hands-on indoor and outdoor galleries (Wikipedia, Fernbank Museum).
attractions.hours: Monday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m., Tuesday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m., Wednesday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m., Thursday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m., Friday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m., Saturday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m., Sunday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m. (Fernbank Forest closes at 4:30 p.m.) (Fernbank Museum)
attractions.admission: Online pricing: Value (Mon-Fri) - Adult $25.95, Senior $24.95, Child $23.95; Standard (Weekends) - Adult $27.95, Senior $26.95, Child $25.95. Tickets at the counter or by phone are an additional $3. Members and children ages 2 and under are Free (Fernbank Museum).
highlights: ['Giants of the Mesozoic (featuring Argentinosaurus and Giganotosaurus)', 'WildWoods and Fernbank Forest outdoor trails', 'A Walk Through Time in Georgia exhibit', "NatureQuest interactive children's exhibit", 'Fantastic Forces STEM gallery', 'Giant Screen Theater films']
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets online in advance to save $3 per ticket compared to counter prices. The museum is a cashless facility, so bring a credit or debit card. Wear comfortable shoes to explore the extensive outdoor trails and forest (Fernbank Museum).
attractions.nearby: Fernbank Science Center, Michael C. Carlos Museum, Jimmy Carter Presidential Library & Museum, Emory University, Cator Woolford Gardens, Olmsted Linear Park (TripAdvisor)

attractions.name: Fernbank Science Center
attractions.description: The Fernbank Science Center is an educational facility featuring a 9,000-square-foot exhibit hall, a 500-seat planetarium, and an observatory. Operated by the DeKalb County School District, it provides hands-on STEM learning experiences and public science programs.

attractions.name: Fox Bros. Bar-B-Q
attractions.description: Fox Bros Bar-B-Q is an iconic Atlanta institution founded by Texas-born twin brothers Jonathan and Justin Fox, who brought their passion for authentic Texas-style barbecue to Georgia in 2007. The restaurant is renowned for its low-and-slow smoked brisket, pulled pork, and unique Southern-Texas fusion dishes like the 'Fox-a-roni'. With a high-energy atmosphere and a popular outdoor patio, it consistently earns top rankings as one of the best barbecue spots in the country. (Official Website, Atlanta Magazine)

(The Atlanta Journal-Constitution)']

Be sure to try the 'Fox-a-roni' (a mix of mac and cheese and Brunswick stew) and save room for the homemade banana pudding. attractions.name: Grant Park Neighborhood
attractions.description: Grant Park is one of Atlanta's oldest and most significant historic districts, renowned for its lush greenery and extensive collection of Victorian-style architecture. The neighborhood centers around the 131-acre namesake park, the city's oldest city-owned green space, which houses major attractions like Zoo Atlanta and the historic Erskine Memorial Fountain. It offers a unique blend of urban natural beauty, historical landmarks, and a vibrant residential community. (Discover Atlanta, Wikipedia)

attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Tripadvisor)

(Grant Park Farmers Market)', 'The site of the Lemuel P. Grant Mansion, the second-oldest house still standing on its original location in Atlanta. (Wikipedia)', 'Home to the annual Grant Park Summer Shade Festival, a major local arts and music event. (Summer Shade Festival)']
attractions.tips: Visit on a Sunday to experience the vibrant Grant Park Farmers Market. The neighborhood is highly walkable, so wearing comfortable shoes is recommended for exploring the historic streets and park trails. For a more quiet experience, visit the park early in the morning before the zoo crowds arrive. (Redfin, Discover Atlanta)

attractions.name: Hammonds House Museum (Official Website)
attractions.description: Housed in a 19th-century Victorian home in the historic West End, Hammonds House Museum is a premier institution dedicated to celebrating and preserving the visual culture of artists from the African Diaspora. Established in 1988 in the former residence of Dr. Otis Thrash Hammonds, the museum features a permanent collection of over 450 works by world-renowned artists such as Romare Bearden and Robert S. Duncanson (Official Website, Discover Atlanta).

Founded by chefs Cody Taylor and Jiyeon Lee, the restaurant is famous for its unique menu items like miso-injected brisket and gochujang-rubbed ribs, all served from a humble location shared with a convenience store (Official Website, Yelp).

attractions.name: Jimmy Carter Presidential Library and Museum
attractions.description: The Jimmy Carter Presidential Library and Museum houses the papers and other materials relating to the administration and life of the 39th President of the United States. Located in Atlanta, Georgia, the museum features exhibits on Carter's early life, his presidency, and his extensive humanitarian work through the Carter Center. Visitors can explore a life-size replica of the Oval Office and learn about key historical events like the Camp David Accords (Official Website).

attractions.tips: Tickets can be purchased in advance online or on-site. Visitors should arrive no more than 15 minutes before their scheduled ticket time. Sealable water bottles are permitted, and free parking is available for guests (Official Website).

The park features over 22 miles of interpretive trails, historic earthworks, and the 1,808-foot Kennesaw Mountain summit, which offers sweeping views of the Atlanta skyline National Park Service, Wikipedia

Opened in 2017, it is renowned for its innovative pinwheel roof design, the massive 'Halo Board' video display, and its status as the first professional sports stadium in North America to achieve LEED Platinum certification.

attractions.description: Spelman College is a private, historically Black, women's liberal arts college in Atlanta, Georgia. Founded in 1881 as the Atlanta Baptist Female Seminary, it is the oldest private historically Black liberal arts institution for women in the United States and a member of the Atlanta University Center consortium (Wikipedia, Spelman About).
attractions.hours: Administrative Offices: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Family Tours: Mon/Wed/Fri 11:00 AM & 2:00 PM; Group Tours: Tue/Thu 10:00 AM, 12:30 PM & 2:30 PM; Museum: Wed-Fri 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Sat by appointment (Spelman Admissions, Spelman Museum)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Saturday (Specific hours vary by facility) (Spelman Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (Campus tours and Museum entry), $3.00 for on-campus parking (Spelman Admissions)
highlights: 'Oldest private historically Black liberal arts college for women in the U.S. ([Wikipedia)', "Founding member of the Atlanta University Center (AUC), the world's largest consortium of African American higher education institutions (Wikipedia)", 'Home to the Spelman College Museum of Fine Art, the only museum in the nation dedicated to art by and about women of the African Diaspora (Spelman Museum)', 'Consistently ranked as the #1 Historically Black College or University (HBCU) in the nation by U.S. News & World Report (Wikipedia)', 'Beautiful 39-acre gated campus featuring historic landmarks and over 25 buildings (Spelman Admissions)']
attractions.tips: Campus tours must be scheduled in advance through the Office of Admissions as self-guided tours are not permitted; visitors should check the academic calendar for blackout dates and college closings (Spelman Admissions, Spelman Admissions Events).
attractions.nearby: Morehouse College, Clark Atlanta University, West End Park, Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Hammonds House Museum (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: State Farm Arena
attractions.description: State Farm Arena is a premier multi-purpose indoor venue located in the heart of downtown Atlanta. It serves as the home for the NBA's Atlanta Hawks and hosts a wide array of global entertainment events, including major concerts and family shows.
attractions.hours: Box Office: Open event days only, starting one hour prior to door time. Arena doors typically open 60-90 minutes before the scheduled start of an event (Box Office Info).
attractions.days_open: Open on event days; see official calendar for specific dates.
attractions.admission: Varies by event; tickets available via Ticketmaster and the State Farm Arena Box Office. Accessible seating is offered in all price ranges.
highlights: "Home venue for the NBA's Atlanta Hawks ([State Farm Arena)", 'Features a massive 360-degree center-hung video board covering over 4,400 square feet (Quick Facts)', 'World’s first TRUE zero-waste certified sports and entertainment venue (Sustainability)', 'Renovated in 2017-2018 to include top-tier amenities, clubs, and luxury suites (Wikipedia)', 'Consistently ranked as one of the best venues in the country by Front Office Sports (Quick Facts)']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to use MARTA public transportation (State Farm Arena/GWCC station) and should pre-purchase parking through ParkMobile if driving. Be sure to review the bag policy before arrival as clear bag requirements are strictly enforced.
attractions.nearby: Centennial Olympic Park, Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Georgia World Congress Center, CNN Center

attractions.name: Stone Mountain Park
attractions.description: Stone Mountain Park is a 3,200-acre destination featuring the world's largest piece of exposed granite, natural beauty, and historical areas. The park offers a wide variety of family-friendly attractions, outdoor recreational activities like hiking and fishing, and seasonal festivals such as the Drone & Light Show. (Stone Mountain Park, Explore Georgia)
attractions.hours: Park Gates: 5:00 AM – 12:00 AM Daily; Summit Skyride: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Attractions: Hours vary seasonally (generally 11:30 AM – 8:00 PM on peak days, closed on select weekdays) (Stone Mountain Park Calendar)
attractions.admission: Adult (Attractions Ticket): $39.99, Child (Attractions Ticket): $36.99, Drone & Light Show Only: Starting at $5.00, Historic Ticket (Select Weekdays): $25.00, Daily Parking: $20.00, Annual Parking: $40.00 (Stone Mountain Park)
highlights: ['Summit Skyride high-speed Swiss cable car to the top of the mountain', 'Scenic Railroad with a five-mile excursion around the park in 1940s cars', 'Dinosaur Explore featuring over 20 life-size, moving prehistoric creatures', 'Nightly Drone & Light Show on the Memorial Lawn (select dates)', 'SkyHike adventure ropes course and Geyser Splash Pad water area', 'Historic Square collection of restored 18th and 19th-century Georgia homes']
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds at popular attractions like the Skyride. Daily parking permits access to natural areas and hiking trails, while an Attractions Ticket is required for theme-park-style fun. Pets are welcome on hiking trails but not in attraction or festival areas. (Stone Mountain Park)
attractions.nearby: Stone Mountain Village, Confederate Hall Historical & Environmental Education Center, Stone Mountain Glass Shop, Art Station, Yellow River Park (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Sweetwater Creek State Park
attractions.description: Sweetwater Creek State Park is a peaceful tract of wilderness only minutes from downtown Atlanta, featuring a 215-acre lake and 15 miles of trails. The park is most famous for the ruins of the New Manchester Manufacturing Company, a textile mill burned during the Civil War, which can be reached via a wooded trail along the stream. (Source: Georgia State Parks)
attractions.hours: Park: 7:00 AM–Dark; Office/Visitor Center: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (Source: Georgia State Parks)
attractions.admission: $10 Daily ParkPass per vehicle (Source: Georgia State Parks), $70 Annual ParkPass available (Source: Georgia State Parks); Guided Mill Tours: $5 plus parking fee (Source: Georgia State Parks Events)
highlights: ['Ruins of the New Manchester Manufacturing Company textile mill', 'George Sparks Reservoir (215-acre lake) for fishing and boating', 'Award-winning, environmentally responsible Visitor Center and Museum', '15 miles of hiking trails, including the popular Red (History) Trail', 'Yurt village for overnight glamping stays', 'Seasonal rentals for kayaks, canoes, and paddleboards']
attractions.tips: Access to the interior of the mill ruins is only permitted on guided tours to protect the historic structure. Parking kiosks are available inside the park, or you can pay via QR code upon arrival. Wear sturdy shoes if hiking the Red Trail, which is rated easy to difficult. (Source: Georgia State Parks, Georgia State Parks Events)
attractions.nearby: Atlanta, GA; Six Flags Over Georgia; Panola Mountain State Park; Pickett's Mill Battlefield State Historic Site; Douglas County Film Trail (Source: Georgia State Parks, Douglas County Film Trail)

attractions.name: The Tabernacle
attractions.description: The Tabernacle is a historic mid-size concert hall located in the heart of downtown Atlanta. Originally built in 1911 as a Baptist church, it was transformed into a premier music and comedy venue for the 1996 Centennial Olympic Games. The venue is celebrated for its stunning architecture, featuring ornate stained-glass windows, a grand pipe organ, and a crystal chandelier, providing an intimate yet grand atmosphere for performances (Wikipedia), Live Nation Special Events).
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Closed (Open only for scheduled events). The box office typically opens 2 hours before showtime on event days, and doors usually open 1 hour before the performance (Tabernacle Official, Audiala).
attractions.days_open: Event days only (Varies based on performance schedule) (Tabernacle Official)
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event, typically ranging from $25 to $150+ for standard tickets; VIP upgrades and premium seating options are available for an additional fee (SeatGeek, Live Nation).
highlights: 'Historic 1911 architecture with neoclassical columns and six ornate red entrance doors ([DittyTV).', 'Interior features a massive crystal chandelier and hand-painted ceilings (Live Nation Special Events).', 'Iconic original grand pipe organ that remains as a centerpiece of the main worship hall (Live Nation Special Events).', 'Tiered wrap-around balconies offering unique vantage points of the early 20th-century architecture (Wikipedia)).', 'Diverse lineup of world-class musical acts and top-tier comedy tours (Tabernacle Official).', 'The Eden Room and Artist Lounge featuring local folk art and hand-painted scenes (Live Nation Special Events).']
attractions.tips: Arrive 30-45 minutes before doors for general admission shows to secure a good spot; be aware that the venue has a strict bag policy (max 12” x 6” x 12”) and is a cashless facility (Ticketmaster Blog, Audiala).
attractions.nearby: SkyView Atlanta, Centennial Olympic Park, Georgia Aquarium, World of Coca-Cola, College Football Hall of Fame (Tripadvisor, Atlanta Downtown).

attractions.name: The Varsity
attractions.description: The Varsity is a historic downtown Atlanta institution and the world’s largest drive-in restaurant, family-owned and operated since 1928. Located right across from Georgia Tech, it is famous for its fast-paced service, iconic 'What’ll ya have?' greeting, and classic American fare like chili dogs and Frosted Orange shakes. The Varsity, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Sunday - Thursday: 10:30am - 8pm; Friday - Saturday: 10:30am - 9pm The Varsity
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on Easter, Thanksgiving, and Christmas) The Varsity
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant); Game Day Parking: $50 per spot The Varsity
highlights: "World's largest drive-in restaurant, covering two city blocks and seating over 800 diners. [The Varsity", "Famous 'What’ll ya have?' greeting from counter staff. The Varsity", 'Iconic menu items including the Frosted Orange (F.O.), chili dogs, and hand-cut onion rings. The Varsity', "Unique 'Varsity Lingo' for ordering, such as 'Walk a Dog' (hot dog to go) or 'Strings' (french fries). The Varsity", 'A major Atlanta landmark and cultural tradition, especially popular on Georgia Tech game days. The Varsity']
attractions.tips: Prepare your order before reaching the counter to keep up with the fast-paced service. Use the 'Varsity Lingo' for a more authentic experience, like ordering 'Strings' for fries or an 'F.O.' for a Frosted Orange shake. Note that while it was famous for car-side service, the main location ended curbside service in 2020. The Varsity, Wikipedia)
attractions.nearby: Georgia Institute of Technology (Georgia Tech), Bobby Dodd Stadium, The Fox Theatre, Bank of America Plaza, Georgia Aquarium, World of Coca-Cola Tripadvisor

attractions.name: The Westview Cemetery, Inc. (Official Website)
attractions.description: Established in 1884, Westview Cemetery is the largest civilian cemetery in the Southeastern United States, sprawling over nearly 600 acres of historic landscape. It serves as a significant landmark in Atlanta, known for its stunning architecture, rolling hills, and as the final resting place of many prominent figures in Georgia's history. The cemetery's crown jewel is the Westview Abbey, a massive mausoleum and chapel featuring exquisite stained glass and intricate Spanish Plateresque-style design. (Wikipedia, Islands)
attractions.hours: Gate Hours: 8:00 a.m. - 5:30 p.m. Monday-Sunday; Office Hours: 9:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Monday-Friday, 9:30 a.m. - 2:00 p.m. Saturday (Closed Sunday); Abbey Hours: 10:00 a.m. - 2:00 p.m. Monday-Saturday (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website), though special guided tours may require tickets (Eventbrite)
highlights: 'Largest civilian cemetery in the Southeastern United States, covering nearly 600 acres ([Wikipedia)', 'The Westview Abbey and Chapel, featuring Spanish Plateresque architecture and extensive stained glass (Atlas Obscura)', 'Historic Romanesque Revival gatehouse designed by architect Walter T. Downing (Andrew J. Bramlett)', 'Final resting place for notable Atlantans including Coca-Cola founder Asa Candler and Robert Woodruff (Wikipedia)', 'Frequently used as a filming location for major films and television series (Islands)', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places and the Georgia Register of Historic Places (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Respect the cemetery as an active place of mourning and maintain a quiet atmosphere. Recreational activities such as jogging, picnicking, bicycling, and dog walking are strictly prohibited on the grounds. Visitors are encouraged to call ahead to ensure the Abbey is open for interior viewing on the day of their visit. (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: The Wren's Nest, Westside Park, Atlanta Beltline Westside Trail, Cascade Springs Nature Preserve (Discover Atlanta, GPSmyCity)

attractions.name: Truist Park
attractions.description: Truist Park is the state-of-the-art home of the Atlanta Braves, offering an intimate 41,000-seat environment with superior sightlines and modern amenities. It serves as the centerpiece of The Battery Atlanta, a massive mixed-use development that provides a year-round destination for shopping, dining, and entertainment. The ballpark blends classic baseball tradition with high-tech features to create a premier fan experience Discover Atlanta, MLB.com
attractions.hours: Non-Game Day Tours (Apr-Sept): Mon-Sat 10 AM, 11 AM, 12 PM, 1 PM, 2 PM; Sun 1 PM, 3 PM. Gamedays: Gates typically open 2 hours before first pitch MLB.com, MLB.com Information Guide
attractions.days_open: Open year-round for tours and events; gamedays follow the MLB schedule MLB.com
attractions.admission: Single game tickets vary by opponent (starting at $10-$20), Stadium Tours: Adults $35, Children (12 & under) $25, A-List/Military $27 MLB.com, MLB.com Tours
highlights: ['Monument Garden: A showcase of Braves history featuring the Hank Aaron statue and the 755 Sculpture.', 'The Battery Atlanta: A 365-day entertainment district integrated directly with the stadium.', 'Mizuno Glove Kiosk: A unique service where fans can rent a baseball glove for free to use during the game.', "Hope & Will's Sandlot: A massive family-friendly area with a zip line and rock-climbing wall.", 'Blue Moon Beer Garden: A right-field rooftop destination featuring craft beers and elevated ballpark food.']
attractions.tips: Pre-purchase parking through the MLB Ballpark app, use Waze for direct routing to your lot, and arrive early to enjoy The Battery before the game Discover Atlanta
attractions.nearby: The Battery Atlanta, Coca-Cola Roxy, Cobb Energy Performing Arts Centre, Cumberland Mall TripAdvisor

attractions.name: Wild Heaven Beer West End
attractions.description: Wild Heaven Beer West End is a 21,000 square foot brewery and garden oasis located in Atlanta’s historic West End neighborhood along the Beltline Southwest Trail (Wild Heaven Beer). The facility features a diverse taproom, an expansive dog-friendly patio, and an on-site Mexican kitchen by Taqueria El Tesoro (Explore Georgia). Visitors can enjoy a wide selection of craft beers, housemade cocktails, and live music at The Garden Club event space (Wild Heaven Beer).
attractions.address: 1010 White St SW, Atlanta, GA 30310 (Wild Heaven Beer)
attractions.hours: Taproom: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Kitchen: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Coffee Bar: Mon-Fri 7:00 AM - 1:00 PM, Sat-Sun 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM (Wild Heaven Beer)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Wild Heaven Beer)
attractions.admission: Free (Wild Heaven Beer)
highlights: 'Located directly on the Atlanta Beltline Southwest Trail ([Wild Heaven Beer)', 'Features "The Garden Club," a 500+ capacity live music and event venue (Wild Heaven Beer)', 'Offers a wide variety of craft beers, including the popular "Emergency Drinking Beer" (The Atlanta Journal-Constitution)', 'In-house food partner Taqueria El Tesoro provides authentic Mexican cuisine (Wild Heaven Beer)', 'Large dog-friendly patio with an outdoor "Emergency Drinking Bar" (Explore Georgia)']
attractions.tips: Visit on Mondays for half-priced cocktails or Tuesdays for $5 Dealer's Choice beers and trivia (Wild Heaven Beer). The patio is dog-friendly, but pets should be kept close; the Coffee Bar opens earlier than the taproom for morning visits (Explore Georgia).
attractions.nearby: Lee+White development, Atlanta Beltline Southwest Trail, Monday Night Garage, Boxcar at Hop City (The Atlanta Journal-Constitution)

attractions.name: World of Coca-Cola
attractions.description: World of Coca-Cola is a dynamic, multimedia attraction located in downtown Atlanta that chronicles the history of the Coca-Cola Company. Guests can explore interactive exhibits, view authentic artifacts, and sample over 100 beverages from around the globe in the popular Taste It! gallery World of Coca-Cola Official Website, World of Coca-Cola FAQ.
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday - Sunday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM World of Coca-Cola Hours, Viator
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day) World of Coca-Cola FAQ, GetYourGuide
attractions.admission: Adult (13-64): $24.95 - $27.95; Senior (65+): $22.95 - $25.95; Youth (3-12): $20.95 - $23.95; Toddler (0-2): Free; Guided Tour: $45.00 World of Coca-Cola Ticketing, World of Coca-Cola FAQ

Gallery: Sample 100+ beverages from the Coca-Cola family across the globe [World of Coca-Cola', 'Vault of the Secret Formula: An interactive exhibit revealing the intrigue behind the legendary secret formula World of Coca-Cola', 'Coca-Cola Polar Bear: A meet-and-greet experience with the iconic brand mascot World of Coca-Cola', 'Beverage Lab: Create your own flavor profile and explore the science behind the drinks World of Coca-Cola', 'The Loft: Home to hundreds of authentic artifacts and vintage advertisements World of Coca-Cola', 'Scent Discovery: An aromatic journey that explores the connection between flavor and memory World of Coca-Cola']

attractions.name: Zoo Atlanta (Zoo Atlanta)
attractions.description: Founded in 1889, Zoo Atlanta is one of the oldest continuously operating zoological institutions in the United States and a proud accredited member of the Association of Zoos and Aquariums (AZA). The Zoo houses over 1,000 animals representing more than 200 species from around the world, featuring one of North America’s largest populations of great apes and the world's first LEED Gold-certified reptile and amphibian exhibit. Located in historic Grant Park, it offers engaging education and conservation experiences for visitors of all ages (Zoo Atlanta, Tripadvisor).
attractions.address: 800 Cherokee Avenue SE, Atlanta, GA 30315 (Zoo Atlanta)
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Last entry 3:30 PM); Saturday-Sunday: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Last entry 4:30 PM) (Zoo Atlanta)
attractions.days_open: Open 363 days a year; closed only on Thanksgiving and Christmas Day (Zoo Atlanta).
attractions.admission: Plan-ahead pricing: Adults (12+) $29.95 - $38.20; Children (3-11) $23.95 - $30.45; Children under 3 FREE. Military: FREE for active-duty, reserves, veterans, and retired; College students: Discounted rate with valid ID (approx. $24.99). Significant discounts for Georgia SNAP and WIC participants (Zoo Atlanta).
highlights: ['Giant Pandas exhibit', 'African Savanna (elephants, giraffes, zebras, and rhinos)', 'Ford African Rain Forest (gorillas and orangutans)', 'Scaly Slimy Spectacular: The Amphibian and Reptile Experience', 'Arthur M. Blank Family Foundation Plaza', 'Treetop Trail aerial playground']
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets in advance online to take advantage of plan-ahead pricing. If it rains, utilize indoor exhibits like the Scaly Slimy Spectacular or the Gorilla Conservation Center. Bring a reusable water bottle to use the bottle-filling stations located throughout the park. Parking is available in public lots for an hourly or daily fee (Zoo Atlanta, CityPASS).
attractions.nearby: Georgia Aquarium, World of Coca-Cola, Centennial Olympic Park, Children's Museum of Atlanta, and historic Grant Park (Zoo Atlanta)

attractions.name: Alliance Theatre
attractions.description: The Alliance Theatre is Atlanta’s national theater, expanding hearts and minds onstage and off. Founded in 1968, it is the leading producing theater in the Southeast and a Tony Award-winning institution known for its high artistic standards. The theater nurtures emerging talent and produces world-class work for both adult and youth audiences across multiple stages. Alliance Theatre
attractions.hours: Box Office: Tuesday – Sunday, 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM (and one hour before showtime); Closed Mondays. Performance times typically include 2:00 PM matinees and 7:30 PM evening shows. Alliance Theatre
attractions.admission: Adult tickets vary by show (typically $25–$75+); Teen tickets (middle/high school): $10 with code TEEN10; Rush tickets: $25 (available 1 hour before showtime); School groups: from $12.50. Alliance Theatre, Alliance Theatre
highlights: ['Tony Award-winning regional theatre legacy with over 140 world premieres.', 'Home to the state-of-the-art Coca-Cola Stage featuring iconic curved wooden architecture.', 'Nurtures emerging writers through the Alliance/Kendeda National Graduate Playwriting Competition.', 'Offers the Bernhardt Theatre for the Very Young, specifically designed for children under five.', 'Significant production history with 11 premieres transferring to Broadway, including "The Color Purple."', 'Key partner of the Woodruff Arts Center, one of the largest arts centers in the world.']
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the show to ensure smooth entry. Use the Alliance Theatre App for digital tickets and a contactless experience. For the best value, check for $25 rush tickets available one hour before curtain. Alliance Theatre
attractions.nearby: High Museum of Art, Atlanta Symphony Orchestra, Piedmont Park, Atlanta Botanical Garden, SCAD FASH Museum of Fashion + Film. Tripadvisor, Discover Atlanta

attractions.name: Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail
attractions.description: The Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail is a multi-use trail and linear greenspace that connects several of Atlanta’s most vibrant neighborhoods. As the first finished section of the BeltLine in the old rail corridor, it serves as a bustling hub for recreation, public art, and commerce, featuring a 14-foot wide paved path. The trail offers a scenic route through the heart of the city, linking major parks and historic markets.
attractions.address: 10th St NE & Monroe Dr NE, Atlanta, GA 30306 (North Entrance) or 650 Irwin St NE, Atlanta, GA 30312 (Old Fourth Ward)
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Explore Georgia)
attractions.admission: Free (Atlanta BeltLine)
highlights: 'Ponce City Market: A historic mixed-use development featuring a massive food hall and shopping ([Lonely Planet).', 'Krog Street Market: A popular destination for artisan food and local retailers (Atlanta BeltLine).', "Public Art: The trail is lined with rotating murals, sculptures, and art installations from 'Art on the Atlanta BeltLine' (Explore Georgia).", 'Historic Fourth Ward Park: A beautiful greenspace with a skatepark and splash pad accessible via the trail (PATH Foundation).', 'Skyline Views: Offers some of the best views of the Midtown and Downtown Atlanta skylines (Tripadvisor).', "Piedmont Park: The trail terminates at the city's premier park, providing easy access to its vast amenities (Wikipedia)."]
attractions.tips: The trail is extremely busy on weekends; cyclists should be mindful of heavy pedestrian traffic. For a faster tour, consider renting a bike or scooter from local vendors like the Atlanta Bicycle Barn. Stay to the right and pass on the left to follow proper trail etiquette. Many restaurants along the trail offer walk-up windows for quick refreshments. (Lonely Planet, Atlanta BeltLine)
attractions.nearby: Piedmont Park, Ponce City Market, Krog Street Market, Historic Fourth Ward Park, Inman Park Neighborhood (PATH Foundation)

attractions.name: Atlanta Beltline (Atlanta Beltline)
attractions.description: The Atlanta Beltline is a transformative urban redevelopment project consisting of a 22-mile loop of multi-use trails, parks, and transit along a historic railroad corridor. It connects 45 intown neighborhoods while featuring the largest free public art exhibition in the Southeast. (Atlanta Beltline About, Discover Atlanta)
attractions.address: 100 Peachtree Street NW, Suite 2300 (Office) / Multiple trail access points citywide
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 6:00am - 11:00pm (Explore Georgia)
attractions.admission: Free Admission (Explore Georgia, Atlanta Beltline FAQ)
highlights: ['Eastside Trail: The most developed section connecting Piedmont Park to Reynoldstown.', 'Art on the Atlanta Beltline: The largest free public art exhibition in the Southeast.', 'Historic Fourth Ward Park: A 17-acre oasis with a splash pad, skatepark, and amphitheater.', 'Atlanta Beltline Arboretum: A "botanical garden on the move" with native plants and trees.', "Westside Trail: A 3-mile paved trail winding through some of Atlanta's oldest neighborhoods."]
attractions.tips: Walk on the right and pass on the left; use a bell or voice to signal passing. Consider visiting the Eastside Trail for the most dining and shopping options, but try the Westside Trail for a quieter experience. Bring water and sun protection as some sections have limited shade. (Atlanta Beltline Visitor Info, Discover Atlanta)
attractions.nearby: Piedmont Park, Ponce City Market, Krog Street Market, Centennial Olympic Park (Atlanta Beltline, Discover Atlanta)

attractions.name: Atlanta Botanical Garden
attractions.description: Since opening in 1976, the Atlanta Botanical Garden has become the "emerald jewel in the crown of Atlanta culture," offering a 30-acre urban oasis in the heart of Midtown (Atlanta Botanical Garden). It features renowned plant collections, spectacular exhibitions like "Niki in the Garden," and unique attractions such as the Kendeda Canopy Walk and Dorothy Chapman Fuqua Conservatory (Atlanta Botanical Garden).
attractions.address: 1345 Piedmont Avenue NE, Atlanta, GA 30309 (Atlanta Botanical Garden)
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Sunday: 9 a.m. – 9 p.m. (Summer hours May 8 – Sept 7); Winter hours (Nov – March) are typically 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. (Atlanta Botanical Garden)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday – Sunday (Closed on Mondays) (Atlanta Botanical Garden)
attractions.admission: Online Weekday: Adult $27.95, Child (3-12) $24.95; Online Weekend: Adult $32.95, Child $29.95; In-person tickets are $2 higher; Members and children under 3 are Free (Atlanta Botanical Garden Tickets). A $5 processing fee applies to online and phone orders (Atlanta Botanical Garden).
highlights: 'Kendeda Canopy Walk (40-foot-high suspension bridge) ([Passport To Eden)', 'Dorothy Chapman Fuqua Conservatory (tropical and desert plants) (Explore Georgia)', 'Fuqua Orchid Center (rare and endangered orchids) (Tripadvisor)', 'Earth Goddess Mosaiculture Sculpture (Passport To Eden)', 'Skyline Garden with views of Midtown Atlanta (Passport To Eden)', 'Storza Woods and Japanese Garden (Justin Landis Group)']
attractions.tips: The Garden is credit/debit card only; cash is not accepted (Atlanta Botanical Garden). Outside food and drinks are prohibited except for water, and picnicking is not allowed on the grounds (Passport To Eden). It is highly recommended to purchase timed tickets online in advance to guarantee entry and save on admission fees (Atlanta Botanical Garden).
attractions.nearby: Piedmont Park, High Museum of Art, Georgia Aquarium, World of Coca-Cola, Zoo Atlanta (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Atlanta Brewing Company (Red Brick Brewing)
attractions.description: Founded in 1993, Atlanta Brewing Company is Georgia's oldest craft brewery, originally known as Red Brick Brewing. It played a pioneering role in the Southern craft beer movement and is famous for its long-standing collaboration with The Vortex Bar & Grill. Although its longtime taproom in the Upper Westside closed in 2022, the brand continues to be a significant part of Atlanta's brewing history under new ownership.
attractions.address: 2323 Defoor Hills Rd NW (Last known location; currently closed)
attractions.hours: Historically: Wed-Thu 4:00 PM – 9:00 PM, Fri 3:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Sat 12:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Sun 1:00 PM – 6:00 PM (Taproom is currently reported permanently closed) (TapHunter, Yelp)
attractions.admission: Free (Historical: $12-$15 for tour and tasting packages)
highlights: "Georgia's first and oldest craft brewery, established in 1993 ([Wikipedia)", "Creator of the iconic 'Laughing Skull' Amber Ale, a collaboration with The Vortex (Atlanta Magazine)", 'Recipient of multiple Great American Beer Festival awards for various craft styles (Authentic Georgia)', "Produced the 'Brick Mason' series, featuring unique high-gravity and barrel-aged beers (Wikipedia)", "Historically hosted one of the city's most popular industrial taproom experiences and tours (AFAR)", 'Successfully rebranded from Red Brick Brewing back to its original name for its 25th anniversary (Beer Street Journal)']
attractions.tips: The physical taproom on Defoor Hills Road is currently closed and plans for a downtown relocation were canceled in 2023; beer enthusiasts should look for their flagship brews at local Atlanta retailers or bars like The Vortex (The Atlanta Journal-Constitution, Metro Atlanta CEO).
attractions.nearby: Monday Night Brewing, Bold Monk Brewing Co., Fire Maker Brewing Company, Scofflaw Brewing Co., The Atlanta BeltLine Westside Trail (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Atlanta Symphony Orchestra
attractions.description: The Atlanta Symphony Orchestra (ASO) is a Grammy Award-winning American orchestra and the cornerstone for musical excellence in the Southeast (Explore Georgia). Established in 1945, the ASO performs more than 200 concerts annually for a combined audience of over half a million people, featuring world-class artistry and diverse programming (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra). The organization is celebrated for its renowned chorus and an impressive list of award-winning recordings (Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Monday: Closed. Note: The Box Office opens 2 hours prior to performance start times and remains open through intermission (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra Box Office).
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by performance; community concerts are often Free (Explore Georgia). Classical and special event tickets typically start from approximately $25-$40 (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra).
highlights: 'Home to the multi-Grammy Award-winning Atlanta Symphony Orchestra Chorus ([Wikipedia)', 'Performs at the prestigious Atlanta Symphony Hall within the Woodruff Arts Center (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra)', 'Hosts the Atlanta Symphony Youth Orchestra and the Talent Development Program for young musicians (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra)', 'Regularly features the Delta Classical Series alongside film scores, jazz, and pop collaborations (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra)', 'Presents annual free community concerts, including popular performances at Piedmont Park (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra News)']
attractions.tips: Patrons are encouraged to arrive at least 30-45 minutes early to allow time for parking and security (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra FAQs). While there is no formal dress code, most attendees wear business casual or cocktail attire. Prepaid parking can be reserved in advance through Ticketmaster or ParkMobile to guarantee a spot in the Woodruff Arts Center garage (Atlanta Symphony Orchestra Directions & Parking). For public transit, take MARTA to the Arts Center Station (N5), which is directly across from the venue.

attractions.name: Fox Theatre (Fabulous Fox)
attractions.description: The Fox Theatre, popularly known as the "Fabulous Fox," is a historic 1920s movie palace and a premier performing arts venue located in the heart of Midtown Atlanta. It is celebrated for its opulent Moorish and Egyptian design, featuring the world-famous "Mighty Mo" Möller pipe organ and an atmospheric auditorium with a "night sky" ceiling of twinkling crystal stars. Today, the 4,665-seat landmark hosts more than 250 performances annually, including Broadway productions, concerts, ballets, and classic film screenings (Fox Theatre, Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: Call Center: Mon-Fri 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM; In-Person Ticket Window: Fri 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Show Days: Opens 3 hours before doors (Fox Theatre)
attractions.days_open: Monday through Saturday (Box Office/Call Center); Sunday for scheduled performances (Fox Theatre)

attractions.tips: Arrive at least 10 minutes early for guided tours, as they start promptly and cover 10 key locations. All guests, including infants, must have a ticket for entry, and security screenings are required for all performances (Fox Theatre, Atlanta Journal-Constitution).

The park features a diverse landscape of rolling hills, scenic trails, and the picturesque Lake Clara Meer, providing a peaceful escape with iconic skyline views. It hosts numerous major festivals and offers a wide array of amenities, including sports fields, playgrounds, and a popular off-leash dog park (Piedmont Park Conservancy, Wikipedia).

attractions.description: Terminal West is a premier 7,000-square-foot music and special events venue located within the historic King Plow Arts Center in West Midtown Atlanta. Originally an iron smelting room in a 1930s plow factory, it has been transformed into a modern performance space that retains its industrial-chic aesthetic with exposed brick and factory features. The venue is known for its high-tech lighting and sound systems, hosting a diverse range of national touring acts across genres like electronic, rock, and indie. Terminal West, AEG Global Partnerships
attractions.address: 887 West Marietta St NW, Suite C, Atlanta, GA 30318-5252 Terminal West, Explore Georgia
attractions.hours: Box Office: Opens 30 minutes before doors for every show. Venue: Doors typically open 1-2 hours before the event start time. Stationside Restaurant: Open during all shows. Terminal West, Ticket Fairy
attractions.days_open: Varies based on event schedule; typically open for shows throughout the week. Terminal West Calendar, Yelp
attractions.admission: Ticket prices typically range from $39 to $94+ depending on the artist and demand. A box office handling fee of $3.50 applies to each ticket purchased at the venue. SeatGeek, Terminal West
highlights: 'Housed in a repurposed 1930s plow factory with a unique industrial-chic atmosphere. [Terminal West', 'Features an outdoor roof deck overlooking the historic train tracks of Westside Atlanta. Discover Atlanta', 'Equipped with state-of-the-art sound and high-end lighting production. Unique Venues', 'Connected to the Stationside restaurant, which serves a full menu during all shows. Terminal West Dining', 'Offers an intimate concert experience with a capacity of approximately 625 standing patrons. Terminal West FAQ', 'Includes two full bars serving a variety of craft beers and cocktails. Discotech']
attractions.tips: The venue is cashless; all bars and the box office accept major credit cards and mobile payments. Arrive early for general admission standing-room-only shows if you want to be closer to the stage. Check the outdoor roof deck for a break from the crowd and views of the train tracks. Parking is available in a nearby deck for a fee (typically $12 on show nights). Terminal West FAQ, Tripadvisor Reviews
attractions.nearby: Goat Farm Arts Center, Atlanta Beltline Westside Trail, Trap Music Museum, Georgia Aquarium, National Center for Civil and Human Rights. Discover Atlanta, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: The Optimist
attractions.description: The Optimist brings a seaside fish camp experience to landlocked Atlantans with a menu centered on high-quality, sustainable seafood and wood-fired fish. Housed in a stylishly renovated former ham-aging house, the restaurant features a vibrant oyster bar, nautical-chic decor, and a variety of coastal-inspired dishes. It is a popular destination for both casual oyster happy hours and upscale special occasion dining. (The Optimist, Atlanta.com)
attractions.hours: Patios & Dining Rooms: Monday - Thursday 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Friday 5:00 PM - 11:00 PM, Saturday 3:00 PM - 11:00 PM, Sunday 3:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Oyster Happy Hour: Monday - Friday 5:00 PM - 6:00 PM, Saturday & Sunday 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM (The Optimist)
attractions.admission: Free (The Optimist)
highlights: 'Extensive oyster bar featuring fresh varieties from the Alabama coast to Maine ([The Optimist)', 'Signature wood-fired dishes including hickory-roasted whole Georgia trout (The Optimist)', 'Award-winning reputation, including being named #7 Best New Restaurant in America by Bon Appétit (Bon Appétit)', 'Unique nautical-chic atmosphere within a historic converted warehouse space (The Optimist)', 'Outdoor patio area featuring a mini-golf/putt-putt green for guests (OpenTable)', 'Popular daily Oyster Happy Hour with specialized pricing and selections (The Optimist)']
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended due to high popularity, especially on weekends (OpenTable). For a more intimate experience, request a table in one of the quieter side rooms as the main dining hall can be energetic and noisy (OpenTable). Don't miss the complimentary warm yeast rolls or the signature corn-milk hush puppies (Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Georgia Aquarium, World of Coca-Cola, SkyView Atlanta, Westside Provisions District, and the Atlanta BeltLine Westside Trail (Tripadvisor, Explore Georgia)

attractions.name: The Woodruff Arts Center (Woodruff Arts Center)
attractions.description: The Woodruff Arts Center is a premier visual and performing arts destination located in Atlanta's Midtown district. It serves as the home for three world-class institutions—the High Museum of Art, the Atlanta Symphony Orchestra, and the Alliance Theatre—and is recognized as the largest arts educator in Georgia (Woodruff Arts Center).
attractions.address: 1280 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA 30309 (Woodruff Arts Center)
attractions.hours: High Museum: Tue–Sat 10 a.m.–5 p.m., Sun 12 p.m.–5 p.m. Alliance Theatre Box Office: Tue–Sun 12 p.m.–5 p.m. Campus General Access: Mon–Sat 9 a.m.–11 p.m., Sun 12 p.m.–5 p.m. (High Museum, Explore Georgia)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Sunday (Museum and Box Office closed Mondays) (High Museum, Alliance Theatre)
attractions.admission: High Museum of Art: $23.50 plus fees (General Admission), Free for Members and Children 5 and under (High Museum). Alliance Theatre and Atlanta Symphony Orchestra: Prices vary by performance, starting as low as $22 (Symphony Hall, Alliance Theatre). Parking: $8–$20 weekdays, $25 nights/weekends (Woodruff Arts Center).
highlights: 'High Museum of Art visual collections ([High Museum)', 'Atlanta Symphony Orchestra performances (ASO)', 'Alliance Theatre productions (Alliance Theatre)', 'Sifly Piazza public art space (Wikipedia)', 'Goizueta Stage for Youth & Families (Woodruff Arts Center)']
attractions.tips: Use the Arts Center MARTA station across the street to avoid high parking fees; High Museum members receive free parking in the on-site garage; Arrive at least 30 minutes early for performances to clear security; Check the official website for 'Free Second Sundays' events (Woodruff Arts Center, High Museum).

attractions.name: Atlanta History Center
attractions.description: Founded in 1926, the Atlanta History Center is a 33-acre destination in Buckhead that explores the city's rich history through award-winning exhibitions and historic houses. The campus features the Atlanta History Museum, historic botanical gardens, and the iconic 1928 Swan House, alongside a rare 19th-century cyclorama painting. Atlanta History Center
attractions.hours: Tuesday–Sunday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Historic houses open at 11:00 AM. Atlanta History Center
attractions.admission: Adult (13+): $29.00 (online), Youth (4–12): $25.00 (online), Child (0–3): Free. Members: Free. Official Tickets
highlights: ['Cyclorama: The Big Picture – A fully restored 138-year-old painting of the Battle of Atlanta.', 'Swan House – An elegant 1928 mansion designed by Philip Trammell Shutze.', 'Smith Farm – Atlanta’s oldest surviving farmhouse, dating back to the 1840s.', 'Goizueta Gardens – 33 acres of curated gardens, woodlands, and trails.', "Atlanta ’96 – An exhibition detailing the city's Olympic and Paralympic legacy.", 'Margaret Mitchell House – The Midtown home where the author wrote Gone with the Wind.']
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets online to save $2 per ticket and skip the line. General admission includes access to the main Buckhead campus and the Margaret Mitchell House in Midtown (if visited within nine days). Allow at least 2–3 hours for a full experience. Atlanta History Center
attractions.nearby: Swan House (onsite), Smith Farm (onsite), Buckhead Village District (approx. 1 mile), Legoland Discovery Center Atlanta (approx. 1.6 miles), Chastain Memorial Park (approx. 3 miles). Discover Atlanta

attractions.name: Buckhead Village District
attractions.description: Buckhead Village District is a premier open-air shopping and dining destination in the heart of Atlanta's Buckhead neighborhood, blending modern retail with a community feel. It features a curated collection of over 40 luxury fashion brands, high-end boutiques, and world-class restaurants anchored in European sensibility and Southern charm. The district offers walkable cobblestone streets, interactive art installations, and lush communal spaces like The Plaza, providing a sophisticated yet welcoming urban oasis.
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 11 AM - 7 PM; Sunday: 12 PM - 6 PM (Individual operator hours may vary)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Over 40 luxury fashion brands and boutiques including Hermes, Dior, and Christian Louboutin', 'Diverse and decadent culinary experiences ranging from casual cafes to Michelin-worthy fine dining', 'Walkable cobblestone streets and European-inspired design in an open-air setting', 'The Plaza and The Veranda, beautifully landscaped communal spaces for relaxation and events', 'Regular interactive art installations and seasonal community events such as the Atlanta Christkindl Market', 'Wellness-focused offerings including boutique fitness studios and spa experiences']
attractions.tips: Dress to impress to fit in with the district's upscale, chic atmosphere. Take advantage of the two convenient underground parking garages (North and South) for easy access, and don't miss the hidden speakeasy-style bars for a unique cocktail experience. Visit on weekends for a more vibrant atmosphere, but check individual store and restaurant hours as they may differ from property hours.
attractions.nearby: Atlanta History Center, Swan House, Phipps Plaza, Lenox Square, Buckhead Theatre, Governor’s Mansion, PATH400 Greenway Trail

attractions.name: Cyclorama: The Big Picture (at Atlanta History Center)
attractions.description: Cyclorama: The Big Picture features the fully restored 'The Battle of Atlanta' painting, a 138-year-old panoramic masterpiece standing 49 feet tall and weighing 10,000 pounds. This immersive experience transport visitors onto the 1864 battlefield through a 360-degree view, enhanced by technology and a theatrical 12-minute presentation (Atlanta History Center).
attractions.hours: Tuesday–Sunday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Closed on Mondays (Atlanta History Center)
attractions.admission: Adult: $29.00, Youth (4-12): $25.00, Child (0-3): Free. Admission is included with general admission to Atlanta History Center (Atlanta History Center).
highlights: "Fully restored 138-year-old 'The Battle of Atlanta' cyclorama painting ([Atlanta History Center)", '360-degree view from a 15-foot-tall stationary viewing platform (Atlanta History Center)', '12-minute theatrical multimedia presentation projected onto the painting (Atlanta History Center)', "Includes the 'Civil War in Georgia' animated map exhibition (Atlanta History Center)", "Features the 'Texas' steam locomotive from the 1862 Great Locomotive Chase (Atlanta History Center)", 'Exhibits on the truths and myths of the Civil War and the history of cycloramas (Atlanta History Center)']
attractions.tips: Look for the 'Clark Gable' soldier in the diorama, leave at least 1.5–2 hours to explore the full campus, and take advantage of free on-site parking (Discover Atlanta). Admission is all-inclusive, granting access to the museum, historic houses, and gardens (Atlanta History Center).
attractions.nearby: Swan House, Goizueta Gardens, Smith Family Farm, Buckhead Theatre, LEGO Discovery Center (Discover Atlanta)

attractions.name: Cabbagetown Landmark District
attractions.description: Cabbagetown is a historic intown neighborhood and former textile mill settlement on the east side of Atlanta, listed on the U.S. National Register of Historic Places. Originally built for workers at the Fulton Bag and Cotton Mill, it is now celebrated for its well-preserved shotgun-style houses, vibrant street art, and strong sense of community. The neighborhood is a designated Landmark District, maintaining a unique, close-knit village atmosphere within the urban city. (Cabbagetown.com, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM (Park Hours) (Creative Loafing)
attractions.admission: Free (Chomp & Stomp)
highlights: ['Historic Fulton Bag and Cotton Mill Lofts', 'Krog Street Tunnel and Forward Warrior street art mural walls', 'Annual Chomp & Stomp Chili Cook-off and Bluegrass Festival', "Carroll Street's eclectic local restaurants and shops", 'Stacks Squares rotating outdoor mural gallery', 'Cabbagetown Park and Community Center hub']
attractions.tips: Parking is extremely limited in this historic area, so visitors are encouraged to use rideshare, bikes, or MARTA. The neighborhood is exceptionally walkable and best enjoyed by foot to appreciate the dense concentration of street art and historic architecture. (Chomp & Stomp, Apartments.com)
attractions.nearby: Historic Oakland Cemetery, Krog Street Market, Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail, Grant Park, Zoo Atlanta (Wikipedia, Apartments.com)

attractions.name: Historic Oakland Cemetery
attractions.description: Oakland Cemetery is Atlanta's oldest public park and a historic Victorian-style garden cemetery featuring 48 acres of gardens, sculpture, and architecture. It serves as a peaceful green space and outdoor museum that reflects the city's history through the stories of over 70,000 residents interred within its grounds (Historic Oakland Foundation).
attractions.hours: Grounds: Dawn to Dusk daily; Visitor Center & Museum Store: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily (Historic Oakland Foundation)
attractions.admission: Free to visit; ticketed admission required for guided tours and special fundraising events (Historic Oakland Foundation).
highlights: 'The original 6-acre plot founded in 1850 ([Wikipedia))', 'Confederate Section containing an estimated 6,900 soldiers and the 65-foot Confederate Obelisk (Wikipedia))', 'The Jewish Grounds, home to the second oldest Jewish burial ground in Georgia (Historic Oakland Foundation)', 'African American Grounds reflecting the era of segregation and the contributions of notable black figures (Wikipedia))', 'Graves of famous residents including Margaret Mitchell, Bobby Jones, and Maynard Jackson (Explore Georgia)', 'The Bell Tower, housing the Historic Oakland Foundation offices and Visitor Center (Atlanta History Center)']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to download the interactive map or use the ParkMobile app for paid parking at the lot on MLK Jr. Dr. SE. Dogs are welcome on leashes, and photography for personal use is permitted while respecting the site's dignity (Historic Oakland Foundation).
attractions.nearby: Zoo Atlanta, Grant Park, King Memorial MARTA Station, MLK Jr. National Historical Park, DAS BBQ (Historic Oakland Foundation, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Inman Park Neighborhood
attractions.description: Inman Park is Atlanta's first planned residential suburb and its first electric trolley neighborhood, established in the late 1880s by Joel Hurt. Characterized by its lush tree-lined streets and extensive collection of restored Victorian-era mansions, the neighborhood is a premier example of garden suburb design. Today, it is a vibrant intown community known for its historic preservation, eclectic dining scene at Krog Street Market, and the popular annual Inman Park Festival.
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Public Area); Specific businesses and Krog Street Market have varying hours (typically 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM or later).
attractions.admission: Free (Public Neighborhood); Inman Park Festival Tour of Homes requires tickets ($25-$35 typically)
highlights: ["Atlanta's first planned residential suburb and electric trolley neighborhood.", 'Home to the largest collection of Victorian-era architecture in Atlanta, including Callan Castle.', 'Direct access to the Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail for walking and biking.', 'Krog Street Market, a premier gourmet food hall in a repurposed 1920s warehouse.', 'Springvale Park, designed by the Olmsted Brothers, offering scenic green space.', 'The annual Inman Park Festival and Tour of Homes, a celebrated three-day community event.']
attractions.tips: Visit during late April for the annual Inman Park Festival to see the historic homes and parade. Use the MARTA Blue or Green lines to the Inman Park/Reynoldstown station for easy access. Explore the area on foot or by bike via the BeltLine to best enjoy the street art and historic architecture.
attractions.nearby: Old Fourth Ward, Little Five Points, Cabbagetown, Poncey-Highland, Jimmy Carter Presidential Library & Museum.

attractions.name: Krog Street Market
attractions.description: Krog Street Market is a vibrant mixed-use development in Atlanta's Inman Park neighborhood, housed in a restored 1920s warehouse. It features a west-coast style food hall with over 20 vendor stalls, several full-service restaurants, and curated retail boutiques. The market is a central hub for intown culture, offering a unique blend of historic charm and modern culinary energy. The Krog District, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Little Five Points Neighborhood (L5P)
attractions.description: Little Five Points is an eclectic, bohemian neighborhood and commercial district on the east side of Atlanta, famous for its alternative culture, vibrant street art, and unique independent businesses. It serves as a major hub for shopping, dining, and live entertainment, often described as the "Haight-Ashbury of the South" and a melting pot of Southern sub-cultures. Official Site, Wikipedia
attractions.address: Moreland Ave NE & Euclid Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30307
attractions.hours: Neighborhood is accessible 24/7; individual business hours typically range from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM, with bars and music venues open later. L5P Community Center
attractions.admission: Free (Public neighborhood access) Yelp

attractions.tips: Parking is extremely limited in the area; visitors are encouraged to use MARTA (Inman Park/Reynoldstown station is a 10-minute walk) or travel by bike. The neighborhood is best explored on foot to appreciate the street art and local character. Official Site

Known for its "no-nonsense" attitude and strictly 21+ atmosphere, it is famous for serving some of the best half-pound burgers in the city. The Little Five Points location is instantly recognizable by its massive "Laughing Skull" entrance, making it a landmark of the neighborhood. The Vortex, Wikipedia

It is celebrated for its award-winning cocktail program and exceptional Southern hospitality in a setting that feels like a hidden gem.

attractions.name: Ebenezer Baptist Church
attractions.description: Ebenezer Baptist Church is a historic landmark and progressive Baptist megachurch in Atlanta, famously known as the spiritual home of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and a cornerstone of the American Civil Rights Movement. Today, it continues its legacy of social justice and community leadership under Senior Pastor Raphael Warnock, serving both as a place of worship and a key site within the Martin Luther King, Jr. National Historical Park. Ebenezer Baptist Church, Encyclopedia Britannica

[NPS, Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Backpacks and large bags are not allowed in the sanctuary. Flash photography is prohibited inside. Large groups should fill out the visitor form on the official website before attending Sunday services. Please respect the sanctuary as a place of worship. Ebenezer Baptist Church
attractions.nearby: Martin Luther King, Jr. Birth Home, The King Center, MLK Jr. National Historical Park Visitor Center, World Peace Rose Garden Ebenezer Baptist Church

attractions.name: Martin Luther King, Jr. Birth Home
attractions.description: The Martin Luther King, Jr. Birth Home is a two-story Queen Anne Victorian house where the civil rights leader was born on January 15, 1929. He lived in the home for the first 12 years of his life, and it has since been restored to reflect its appearance during that era. It serves as a central landmark within the Martin Luther King, Jr. National Historical Park. (National Park Service)
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (National Park Service)
attractions.days_open: Daily (except Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day) (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: ['Authentic 1930s-era restoration of the interior furnishings and decor', "Dr. King's childhood bedroom where he and his siblings were born", "Guided tours led by National Park Rangers sharing stories of King's early life", 'Classic Queen Anne Victorian architecture built in 1895', 'Historic location in the heart of the Sweet Auburn neighborhood']
attractions.tips: Arrive at the Visitor Center at 450 Auburn Ave NE as early as possible (ideally at 9:00 AM) to claim free, first-come, first-served tour tickets, as they often sell out early in the day. Photography is strictly prohibited inside the home. The facility is wheelchair accessible via a ramp and an interior elevator. Note that the Birth Home may be under renovation; check the official site for current status of interior tours. (National Park Service)
attractions.nearby: Historic Ebenezer Baptist Church, The King Center (Freedom Hall), Fire Station No. 6, World Peace Rose Garden (National Park Service)

attractions.name: Ponce City Market
attractions.description: Ponce City Market is a vibrant mixed-use development housed in the historic 2.1 million-square-foot former Sears, Roebuck & Co. building. It serves as a community hub featuring a world-class food hall, a diverse array of local and national retail shops, modern office spaces, and a rooftop amusement park. Located in the heart of the Old Fourth Ward, it is directly linked to the Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail Ponce City Market, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 675 Ponce De Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30308-1884 Ponce City Market, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Retail: Mon-Sat 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Food Hall: Mon-Sat 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM Ponce City Market

highlights: ['Central Food Hall featuring a diverse global collection of restaurants and eateries.', 'Direct access to the Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail for walking and cycling.', 'Skyline Park on the roof, offering mini-golf, carnival games, and skyline views.', 'Historic 1926 architecture preserved through one of the largest adaptive reuse projects in the South.', 'Curated retail experience with a mix of national brands and local artisan boutiques like Citizen Supply.']
attractions.tips: Visit on weekdays to avoid large weekend crowds and make the most of the Food Hall. Consider arriving via the BeltLine by bike or on foot to avoid parking fees. Making a reservation at the rooftop restaurant, 9 Mile Station, often includes a complimentary ride to the roof Discover Atlanta, Local Adventurer
